message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the deep 328-basket render RELIABLY COMPLETE — no deadlock, no degrade-to-seed — keeping full coverage + faithfulness + agentic. Per the completed speed investigation (/home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md FINAL SYNTHESIS): the clean run is already ~24min (near floor); there is NO big legal speedup on the clean path. The two REAL wins are P0 (ship the safe non-deadlocking compose config so the 16-way HANG becomes a clean completion) and P1 (fix the degrade-to-seed bad tail). DO NOT re-attempt the disqualified levers (parallelize react-loop turns — blocked by the serial decide dependency; fetch cache; more compose/section concurrency; turn caps; basket-worker sharding at scale — each deadlocks or drops coverage or drifts verdicts).
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (1) A FULL 328-basket agentic deep render COMPLETES with NO deadlock and NO SIGKILL, cp4_used=agentic (NOT degraded-seed), faithfulness PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0), ~328/329 baskets rendered, in ~24min. (2) A startup GUARD (assert) refuses the deadlocking config (PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 or side-judge sem>=48) until a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B has certified it — so the deadlock can never ship again. (3) The bad tail is fixed: a run that would have tripped the 900s wall on a mega-fetch no longer DEGRADES to seed (it completes agentic, even if slower). Prove each with a real run + numbers.
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "PROFILED the 43.2min anomaly from step3's log timestamps (logs/step3_full328_render.log): the +1140s vs the 1449.7s baseline is NOT compose. Phase breakdown = init+outline(turns=1) 161s / content-dedup 31s / CREDIBILITY member-verify 1020s / compose+strict_verify+repair 1382s. The 1020s pass ran SERIALLY (PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT=1) and BANKED at its wall*0.85 soft deadline having verified only 207/997 members.",
    "PROVED the 1449.7s 'baseline' is apples-to-oranges: its log (logs/step4_route_all_compose.log) has ZERO psl_gov_suffixes / route-all-baskets / F1-route lines and 0 debate_con_basket_consolidation events (step3 has 16) — it never threaded gov_suffixes, so it ran NEITHER the credibility member-verify pass NOR the full-coverage basket consolidation. The 24min figure was measured on the pre-gov_suffixes path, not the full-coverage mission workload.",
    "Attributed compose 1382s vs 514s to corpus density + full coverage: route_all consolidates all 328 baskets (16 debate_con events vs baseline 0) over 9-11 sections/192 verify sentences (vs 8/133). Inherent to the coverage requirement, not a serial-hotspot regression.",
    "LANDED the fix (commit 67b0151, scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py setdefault, env-overridable, faithfulness-neutral): parallelize the ISOLATED pre-compose credibility member-verify (PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT=16 + PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_SIDE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY=16 phase-only override, wall 600). This phase runs entirely BEFORE compose (no PARALLEL_SECTIONS x BASKET_WORKERS nesting) so it is NOT the deadlock the guard protects against; the credibility_pass_concurrency ctx restores the compose-time cap of 8 before compose starts.",
    "EXERCISED the fix at unit level: _run_member_verifies 48-task probe = 15.9x speedup at conc=16, verdict-IDENTICAL to serial; parallel+tripped-deadline banks real verdicts and sentinel-fills with no None leaked.",
    "STRENGTHENED criterion (3) with a real-code-path test (commit af48c41, tests/test_outline_agent_degrade_tail_grace.py, 3/3 pass): drives the ACTUAL run_outline_agent_or_legacy wait_for->degrade branch, proving the SAME overshooting final turn degrades under grace=0 (cp4_used='agentic-degraded-seed') but COMPLETES agentic under the shipped grace=600 (cp4_used='agentic').",
    "Re-verified criterion (2): guard suite tests/test_compose_config_guard.py 15/15 pass; guard is orthogonal to the credibility knobs (checks only PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 and PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY>=48)."
  ],
  "commits": [
    "67b0151",
    "af48c41"
  ],
  "files": [
    "scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py",
    "tests/test_outline_agent_degrade_tail_grace.py"
  ],
  "exercised": "\"Fix exercised three ways: (1) unit probe of _run_member_verifies = 15.9x speedup verdict-identical + deadline-bank safe (no None leaked); (2) LIVE full render mid-flight confirms the credibility phase collapsed 1020s->329s and COMPLETED 997/997 with zero bank (concurrent I-beatboth-008 disclosures + entailment-judge activity at 07:08:31 confirm the parallel path engaged); (3) guard 15/15 + new degrade-tail real-code-path test 3/3.\"",
  "measurement": "\"See exercised: credibility phase 1020s->329s (live), full 997 corroboration, zero bank; end-to-end wall pending.\"",
  "open": [
    "End-to-end wall of the credibility-fixed full render is not yet captured (compose at 2/9 sections when the round was cut). The final [gen] elapsed / cp4_used=agentic / leaked_[CITE:ev]=0 / baskets>=328 confirmation lines are pending — the render is still running in the background (pid 1258518).",
    "The ~24min target is likely UNREACHABLE for THIS full-coverage corpus even with the credibility fix: the inherent compose is ~1382s (route_all 328-basket consolidation + 9-11 sections/192 verify sentences), so the honest floor is ~28-33min. The 1449.7s 'baseline' was a lower-coverage (pre-gov_suffixes) workload, not the mission's 328-basket requirement.",
    "Criterion (3) real-RUN proof (a live mega-fetch tripping the 900s wall) remains impossible on this pre-built corpus; strengthened to a real-code-path unit proof instead of only standalone asyncio."
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS"
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
"Given the MEASURED breakdown — the +1140s is 1020s credibility member-verify (now fixed to 329s, faithfulness-neutral) plus ~868s INHERENT full-coverage compose (route_all 328-basket consolidation, 16 debate-con events the pre-gov_suffixes 1449.7s baseline never paid) — and given the 24min baseline provably did NOT render full 328-basket coverage: should the mission's ~24min target be formally RESTATED to the honest ~28-33min floor for this full-coverage corpus (compose-bound, all faithfulness gates intact), or do you want me to next attack the inherent compose repair/regen churn (the kept_fraction<0.40 retries) — the only remaining lever — accepting it risks the coverage/faithfulness the retries exist to protect?"

PRIOR ROUNDS (for continuity):
[
  {
    "r": 1,
    "meas": "FULL 328-basket agentic deep render, real end-to-end run (outputs/step3_full328_render, exit 0): WALL=2589.7s (43.2min). NO deadlock, NO SIGKILL/OOM (0 killed/oom/deadlock/traceback in log; main thread stayed in ep_poll with IO byte-counters rising throughout — +22KB write/+10KB read per 30s sample). cp4_used=agentic (degraded_to_seed=False, turns=1, degrade_reason=''). Faithfulness PASS: leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, unresolved_markers=[]. baskets=329 (>=328), 11 sections kept / 0 dropped, 2394 verified words, 60 bib entries. Guard: 15/15 pytest + proven refusal at the real generate_multi_section_report entry point (workers=16/sem=48 -> UnsafeComposeConfigError before any work). Degrade fix: behavioral asyncio proof (run overshooting wall by 300s/466s degrades under grace=180, completes agentic under 600).",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Do NOT pursue the workers=2-4 A/B (the 'timeout-guarded side-judge acquire' is the disqualified JudgeSlotTimeout fail-closed sentinel — drops sentences, I2/faithfulness breach; and sharding collapses to serial at safe judge caps per speed_investigation P3). Instead, explain the +1140s compose anomaly on the safe path: re-run the step3 compose with PG_COMPOSE_TIMING=1 (the instrument Opus already landed in commit 3ff288c, multi_section_generator.py — default-off, byte-identical) and diff per-section/per-basket compose wall + strict_verify wall against the 1449.7s baseline's D-phase (~514s). Attribute the delta to (a) corpus density of cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json (997 ev rows, 192 verify sentences — if so, restate the honest target for THIS corpus with numbers) vs (b) a fixable serial hotspot (side-judge sem=8 429-backoff vs baseline 4, regen retries, or a regression from the surfaced-stats/off-loop changes). Only after that breakdown decide if ~24min needs any concurrency at all — the evidence says it does not."
  }
]

DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? ((1) A FULL 328-basket agentic deep render COMPLETES with NO deadlock and NO SIGKILL, cp4_used=agentic (NOT degraded-seed), faithfulness PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0), ~328/329 baskets rendered, in ~24min. (2) A startup GUARD (assert) refuses the deadlocking config (PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS>1 or side-judge sem>=48) until a FULL-328 verdict-identity A/B has certified it — so the deadlock can never ship again. (3) The bad tail is fixed: a run that would have tripped the 900s wall on a mega-fetch no longer DEGRADES to seed (it completes agentic, even if slower). Prove each with a real run + numbers.)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
commit 67b01511bcbaf1c79184aa8c37a1abfa70e36aa7
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sun Jul 12 07:03:45 2026 +0000

    P1-SPEED: parallelize the isolated pre-compose credibility member-verify pass
    
    ROOT-CAUSE of the 43.2min (2589.7s) vs 24.2min (1449.7s) gap, MEASURED from the
    phase timeline in logs/step3_full328_render.log:
      init+outline(turns=1)          161s
      content dedup + basket build    31s
      CREDIBILITY member-verify     1020s  <== ANOMALY (207/997, SERIAL, banked@wall*0.85)
      compose+strict_verify+repair  1382s
    
    The +1140s is NOT in compose. Threading gov_suffixes to lift route_all basket
    utilization ALSO activates the ADVISORY credibility corroboration pass, which ran
    SERIALLY (PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT=1 default) and banked at 1020s having
    verified only 207/997 members. The 1449.7s baseline NEVER ran this pass (it did not
    thread gov_suffixes -> credibility degraded-to-unscored). Compose 1382s vs 514s is
    corpus density (11 sections/192 verify sentences vs 8/133, higher drop -> more
    kept_fraction<0.40 repair retries), not a serial-hotspot regression.
    
    FIX (driver setdefault, env-overridable, faithfulness-neutral): the credibility pass
    runs ENTIRELY BEFORE compose (ISOLATED flat phase, no PARALLEL_SECTIONS x
    BASKET_WORKERS x inner-TPE nesting), so bounding its own loop concurrency is NOT the
    multiplicative compose oversubscription the deadlock guard protects against.
      PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT=16        (parallelize the member-verify loop)
      PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_SIDE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY=16 (raise side-judge cap FOR THIS PHASE
         ONLY; credibility_pass_concurrency restores the compose-time cap of 8 first)
      PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_WALL_S=600
    ADVISORY/UNDERCOUNT-only: strict_verify / 4-role D8 / span-grounding untouched.
    Exercised: _run_member_verifies 48-task probe = 15.9x speedup, verdict-IDENTICAL to
    serial; parallel+tripped-deadline banks real verdicts + sentinel-fills, no None leaked.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py | 18 ++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 18 insertions(+)

diff --git a/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py b/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py
index a5d9285..d17f317 100644
--- a/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py
+++ b/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py
@@ -151,6 +151,24 @@ async def main() -> int:
     os.environ.setdefault("PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS", "1")
     os.environ.setdefault("PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY", "8")
     os.environ.setdefault("PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS", "3")
+    # P1-SPEED (2026-07-12) — collapse the ISOLATED pre-compose credibility member-verify pass.
+    # ROOT-CAUSE of the 43min (2589.7s) >> 24min (1449.7s) gap, MEASURED from the phase timeline in
+    # logs/step3_full328_render.log: threading the PSL gov_suffixes (below) to lift route_all basket
+    # utilization ALSO activates the ADVISORY credibility corroboration pass. On this 997-member corpus
+    # that pass ran SERIALLY (PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT default=1) and BANKED at its
+    # wall*0.85 soft deadline = 1020s, verifying only 207/997 members — a full +1020s phase the 1449.7s
+    # baseline NEVER ran (it did not thread gov_suffixes -> credibility degraded-to-unscored, skipped).
+    # This pass runs ENTIRELY BEFORE compose (an ISOLATED flat phase — NO PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS x
+    # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS x inner-TPE nesting), so bounding its OWN loop concurrency is NOT the
+    # multiplicative compose oversubscription the deadlock guard protects against. Parallelize the
+    # member-verify loop and raise the side-judge cap FOR THIS PHASE ONLY (the designed I-deepfix-001
+    # box2 lever; credibility_pass_concurrency RESTORES the compose-time cap of 8 before compose starts).
+    # Faithfulness-neutral & UNDERCOUNT-only: the pass is ADVISORY (strict_verify / 4-role D8 /
+    # span-grounding are untouched); verifying MORE members in LESS time yields STRICTLY MORE
+    # corroboration than the 207-serial run and far more than the baseline's zero. All env-overridable.
+    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT", "16")
+    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_SIDE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY", "16")
+    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_WALL_S", "600")
     # STEP 2: topic-driven, synthesis-enabling structure. Facet outline (thematic sections emerge
     # from the evidence) + the general research-report skeleton (intro / thematic bodies /
     # cross-study synthesis+contradictions / conclusions+gaps). GENERAL structural flags — they
